Class ReportsPanel

java.lang.Object
ui.manager.ReportsPanel
public class ReportsPanel extends Object
Handles the generation and display of business intelligence reports.

This panel provides various reporting tools including sales trends, inventory usage, and financial "X" and "Z" reports. It communicates directly with the database to run complex aggregations and updates the UI's table view with the results.

  • Constructor Details

    • ReportsPanel

      public ReportsPanel(JFrame frame, DatabaseManager db_Manager, GUI gui, ManagerPanel manUI)
      Constructs the ReportsPanel with required database and UI references. * @param frame The main application JFrame.
      Parameters:
      db_Manager - Instance of the database helper class.
      gui - The main GUI controller.
      manUI - The parent manager panel.

  • Method Details

    • ManagerReportsPage

      public void ManagerReportsPage()
      Builds the Reports user interface.

      Creates a layout containing a results table and a grid of buttons. Each button is linked to a specific SQL query (e.g., Top Selling Items, Product Usage, etc.) via Lambda ActionListeners.

    • runZReport

      public void runZReport(LocalDate today, DefaultTableModel model)
      Generates a "Z-Report" which finalizes the day's sales and resets totals.

      A Z-Report provides a final snapshot of the day's revenue and order counts. Warning: Running this method resets the daily aggregation tables (daily_report_totals and report_hourly) to zero for the next business day.

      * @param today The current date to finalize.
      Parameters:
      model - The table model where the final summary will be displayed.

    • updateReportTotalsForOrder

      public void updateReportTotalsForOrder(float orderTotal, Timestamp orderTime)
      Updates the real-time aggregation tables whenever an order is completed.

      This method is called by the cashier interface after a successful checkout. It updates:

      • daily_report_totals: Increments order count and adds to total revenue.
      • report_hourly: Tracks sales spikes throughout the day.
      It uses an "UPSERT" (Insert on Conflict Update) strategy to handle database records efficiently.

      * @param orderTotal The total price of the order just placed.
      Parameters:
      orderTime - The timestamp of when the order occurred.
